subject_options: a Thai Noodle Bar — making 102 reviews work harder
One small addition that supports more bookings
Something worth a look for a Thai Noodle Bar
email_body:
I came across a Thai Noodle Bar while researching well-built restaurant sites in Connaught, and yours stands out technically — which is why I wanted to reach out with something specific rather than a generic pitch.

You have 102 Google reviews averaging 4.6 stars, which is a genuinely strong reputation signal for a restaurant in Westport. If those reviews are not currently visible on the site itself, first-time visitors making a booking decision are not seeing them at the moment that matters most.

I work with hospitality businesses on a fixed-scope package that surfaces curated review excerpts near the booking area and confirms local schema is in place for Westport dining searches — delivered within one to two weeks, no ongoing commitment required.

If it would be useful, I am happy to send over a one-page summary of what is involved, or we could have a ten-minute call this week to talk through it.

Best regards
[Your name]
cta: If it would be useful, I am happy to send over a one-page summary of what is involved, or we could have a ten-minute call this week to talk through it.
follow_up_body:
I wanted to follow up briefly on the email I sent last week about a Thai Noodle Bar. The core idea was a short, fixed-scope piece of work to surface your Google reviews on-site and confirm local search markup is in place for Westport dining queries — something contained and straightforward.

I appreciate timing may not be right, but I wanted to ask — is this something you would consider over the coming months, or is there someone else on the team who handles the website I should direct this to?
personalisation_tokens_used: Westport location used to frame local search and booking intent
